star_1.text: So I still don't understand why you get epic characters as rewards in the events when they don't do anything... I've played the 8 bit one like 12 times and gotten so ma y epic cards and when I start its the same ... 3 and level up the same. The best thing is your reply will say we appreciate the feedback. I've lowered to 1 star from 2

It tried to trick me into giving it a good review. At the fifth level it steps up ads and keeps asking for me to pay money for a bonus. Then it halts progress to a stand still by restricting workers. Then upgrading becomes impossible so you need to put money in. Not worth the time.

1) watch an ad to double your AFK money, watch the full add and no double. You have to watch ANOTHER ad. 2) some ads take over a minute with no skip (talking to you Cash Frenzy and Blackout Bingo) 3) you won't get the cards you need, but you can buy them for the low price of $20 per EACH LEVEL 4) been on the same level for a week because I can't get the cards I need without spending real money. 5) the game is forgettable as I set my phone down during the 1 minute ads, and forgot I was playing

Doesn't have enough levels to unlock all the characters, items to upgrade that you can't get the cards for because you can't replay levels, once every few months they add one level which lasts 10 minutes then you have to wait months for one more level, days between events so half the time this game is unplayable

I am stuck still in the tutorial by a glitch and it is impossible to correct. I can't even go into settings to see my ID or contact support. I've tried all the regular steps and even uninstalled, cleared cache and memory, but it's still stuck.

Poor implementation right off the bat, installed the game went to play it and what happens.....get a critical update message, click to get the update...... and according to the page on the play store there is NO...I REPEAT NO UPDATE. I could not even get past this after 5 attempts. Even uninstalled and reinstalled it SAME FRIGGIN THING. Shame, did you even play test this???

Basically nothing to do, art style feels cheap and lazy, premise is dumb, story is so bad I gave up caring but it keeps forcing you to skip through text every time you complete a task and after a few resets it becomes so slow it's not worth playing at all anymore

It's just a boring game. The story was repetitive. You kept having to remake your arcade every time you leveled up. I didn't want all that. I just wanted to make an arcade, collect money and build it bigger and bigger.

Another level based idle game. Get the first two gens, wipe progress. Get 3 gens, wipe progress. Get 4 then wipe again. Just use prestige mechanics. Restarting for 1 more gen over and over is such a stale idle mechanic and cheap way to reuse content very heavily.

Was okay till I got to episode 3 and one of the tasks is to collect 250 pizza. I had already maxed out the arcades and there doesn't seem to be any other way to get pizza then to pay $ Edit: it's been a few days and I have yet to see the "pizza party pack"

Get rid of the levels. Restarting at a forced point makes the game pointless. Nothing you make matters at all because you can't use anything you get. Leveling up means you lose everything; ro you people not understand that leveling up is supposed to be good?

I've seen alot of money hungry game makers but wow this game is #1 on the list u literally will have to sit here for hours to complete tasks or spend money if u want to advance I thought it was gonna be pretty good game but im deleting it already after a few hours
Prepare for the game to ask about " How about you check out this crazy deal " ever 5-30mins, it's rather annoying, other than that it's great but the constant nag to but deals is what got the rating

Hey! I literally JUST downloaded the game a moment ago, but it keeps telling me there's an update I need to install! That doesn't seem right, does it? Edit: Uninstalling and reinstalling the game didn't help.
It's an idle game with a nice and cool theme. Very boring and tedious overall though. Seeing the art and styling is not worth having to play this. Strongly recommend don't download.

I installed the game and Google play says it's fully updated. The second I start the game up it asks me to download an update inside the game itself that's 6.6mb big. Not happening. If you have updates out them through the play store process.

Installed the app, and upon launching it, the app tells me there's an update to install. Clicking install takes me to the app store, and since I've just installed it there's no update. Can only play or uninstall, so guess what I did? Uninstalled.

My game keep.revert to scenario 7 after complete 3 days event. Already stage 8, back to 7 again. Theb already reach stage 10, back to 7 again. Edit : already send error report in game twice. But still no.solution till now

They lost all credibility. Their limited offers are such rubbish where if you have a timer and another pops up, you lose that one and get stuck with the new one. Then to add insult to injury they refused to rectify the situation because it cost me being #1 on leaderboard and they refused to offer any kind of refund. Don't even waste your time on these trash heep losers who can't even be bothered for the simplest of things. I used to believe in them but now they are better off being shut down
Super annoying. Constant interruptions with awful cutscenes. Pestering you about spending real money on a game that stole its character art from a TV show. Everything about this is awful.

star_2.text: I like the style of this game but once you get past the first few episodes it becomes a slog. I have trouble setting up the automation because you have to level up certain characters and can only hope you have enough cards to level them up. Throw in limited events where you might not be able to set up idle automation unless you pay real gems which you primarily get with real money and this game shows its greed. Not to mention every time a new thing happens I get a new "Limited deal" wanting $.

The art style is nice. I don't care about the characters and I wish there was a skip button for the many, many cutscenes. Another huge issues is the game forces you to click into a micro transaction screen and when you click "No" there is a second screen asking "Are you sure?" Then you have to click "Yes" again to get past that screen! Honestly ruins the whole experience. Fix this because this is some bull.

I don't mind the advertisements but I really don't like the fact that when it's an event starts within 5 minutes, there are already people that are far more advanced in the event. Beyond just spending money which means there's hacking in the developers aren't doing anything about it because it's not the first time not even the third time. It's too bad cuz I really enjoyed this game and now I don't even want to play it anymore. I have sent devs several emails about the problem with no reply.

It' an ok game for the story line, I guess. Buggets problem is with multiplayer events. Sure, you can win great prizes after ranking high enough in event, but It's all pay to win. I like spending a few dollars here and there on games, but when the competition can outspend you to win prizes less than what they're worth of what is spent to win? No thanks. I'll stick to the story.

The game is very short. Once you get to level 25, all that left are the events, with the weekend events being the largest. On the developer facebook, they have said they will eventually get back to the game, but for now they will concentrate on the weekend events. So, you will have fun until level 25, and then you will only be interested in it on the weekends, at least until it becomes repetitive, and it will.

Fun idea. But the tutorial's SUPER annoying. No, I don't want to watch a damn ad to double my income for two measly hours. I'd watch one eventually, but not if you're trying to force me. Screw that. Then you can't skip the cutscenes. Who does that? There're too many problems with this to list in 500 characters. Uninstalling.

Been enjoyable so far. But just now I logged in without receiving any rewards from the event that went on for a few days. Kind of felt like my time was wasted since no credit means no progress in the main game. Also the "don't miss out on this deal" is unnecessary and constant with any progress

Was very excited about this title, but during one of the tutorials where it was instructing me how to use its premium currency, the menu and the tutorial window clashed and I could no longer interact with the screen at all. Closing the app did not fix the issue, uninstalling and reinstalling did not fix the issue. I was stuck with an unplayable game after about 2 hours of play. I liked the aesthetic and approach to the idle genre, but disappointed that I couldn't play it.

This is actually a surprisingly well-conceived example of the idle tycoon-style game, but there are a couple of things that raise a red flag for me. First off is the existence of multiple currency types (tokens, diamonds, pizza, cards), all of which can be manipulated to create artificial bottlenecks in advancement. The second is that there's no way to eliminate ads; rather the only things for sale in the store are tiny incremental boosts in currency. Ads become crucial to maintain momentum.

The game is ok but only for first few levels. Then there is no progres if you do not spend real money. Nice graphics and idea but it is getting boring withing few hours if you are not willing to invest real money in it.

Something is up lately and the game is becoming increasingly less fun and less of an idle game. The last few events have failed to give you the cards needed for the most basic goal progression and have turned it into pay pay pay. I can't even automate half the business becasue thr only way to do so is buy thr bundles or buy more gems. The cards needed to do the most basic functions are locked behind a pay wall.

Microtransactions galore. I've easily plunked like $200 in this game. I liked the style and gameplay enough I was overlooking it. But today alone I paid almost $30 to just advance a bit in a side quest. The two stars are because the story is kind of fun. The clear slowdown when you don't pay is evident. ETA: I got a notification saying the developers replied to this response and I see no response. Very confused.

Takes too long to get into. Paywall is aggressive and forceful. Might have kept it if the game moved a little better and the storyline was worth paying for. This game gets a D- overall. If I am going up against paywalls, I will stick with Mighty Party. Uninstalling.
You can soft lock the game by selling cards for pizza, by not following the missions exactly, and by miss pressing in the shop. I've had to clear data and restart 3 times.
Glitches on its own tutorial so it will freeze out and become unplayable during the time skip section. Also idk why you think that needs the forced tutorial thing
Many times I don't get credit for watching ads. Also, rewards being nerfed after initially acquiring an item make trying for a high rank in an event pointless.
Was fun at first but got very boring fairly quickly. Every level is practically the same...only difference is each level is slower so it takes longer to complete
Slow with a lot of B's storylines that don't really explain anything. You go back, play for a minute and wait....and wait...and wait. Could be good if levels weren't so slow unless you pay to play.
Very boring! Too slow! Ya'll need two leader-boards. One for people who buy stuff. (CHEATERS!) Another for people who play without buying extras. (The fair way to play, w/out 'cheating'!)
Its a fine game but it constantly interrupts itself making it unplayable. Just let people upgrade things, stop trying to talk to us about every little thing

star_3.text: I'm tired of rating this game over and over again. Im not sure why it keeps getting deleted. 3 stars is all im giving it. The forced "would you like to buy this special deal" every 5 minutes is annoying. Game play itself isn't too bad, but does slow down considerably after a few rounds. After that its a waiting game, unless of course you dump money into it.

I made it to level 3. Struggled to make it that far. I get where it's going, but there is to much dialog and not much gameplay. You just sit there and wait for enough coins to do anything. At least other games have mini games or a clicker/tap feature to speed things up. I'm sure there are people who will enjoy this game, but it's just not for me.

Overall a decent idle, but outside of the "story" it's not really any different. The ad rewards aren't worth the effort and the scale jumps insanely high real early. Been two days of constant playing and ads, still can't upgrade a low level character. First few hours are awesome, but this has turned into log on once a day if that. My advice, play to level 8, then uninstall.

Game gets boring very quickly as the time for any progress to be made nearly doubles unless you start dumping into microtransactions. Unable to get enough "Pizza" to upgrade characters or game efficient enough to flatten the heavy incline of time needed to progress. Art style is really nice and all the references are on point. Storyline seems like it'd be interesting but after a certain point you're more concerned with completing goals that you forget there was a story to begin with.

The game has been fairly fun, but I've encountered a game breaking bug that has halted my ability to play, apparently at some point I traded some cards that I needed and now a tutorial pop up to use those cards won't let me do anything but try and upgrade, with the cards I don't have, can't buy cards, can't do anything to fix, would be 4 or five star if fixed

It's a lot like a lot other idle games. it's a good time waster, and it's good if you bounce between this and a few others you enjoy. but getting 'pizza' to upgrade is so slow coming, it seems like they really want you to spend real money to progress once you get past the tutorial. Not to mention I was forced to use diamonds (paid currency) to do an 'hour skip' when I didn't want to. Maybe add some events or like those ads where I can get pizza or something.

Game's fun, the references are generally on point, and I enjoy the quirky storyline. However...it takes far too long to get the pizza/whatever required to upgrade things, you can get stuck because you can't get the required cards, there is no dang way you can get to the "end" of LTEs without paying, and the game stops abruptly at level 25. Devs, you gotta make main game progression a priority. That should never take a back seat to LTEs. The rewards from the LTEs don't matter otherwise.

It's a cool concept and I love the way everything is designed. It's easy to play and fun, but I have one issue with this game. It's a bit agressive with in-app purchases, and promotions. Everytime I move on to a new episode it shoves discount game promotions in my face at least 2-3 times in 30 minutes. Other than that, I like this game and how you've done it, just calm down with the purchase pushing...

Games alright, graphics are decent and the idles not that bad like most idle games are. The only thing that's super obnoxious about this game is the dialogue and some of the characters within the game. But I couldn't stand it to keep the game installed on my phone was that bad for me.

Not a bad game of the type and I like the 80s parodies. One thing I can think of off top of my head that would make it better and would make me rate it better is the free party packs. Especially during the events it would be great if like 2 could stack. We're missing out on potentially 3 party packs when we are at work or sleeping (if sleeping 8hrs) which is alot especially during the 24 hr events. Unless someone wants to wake up every 2 hrs to open one so timer can start for next free one.

It's a good game, lots of side events to go with the main levels. It would be better if you could pay for ad free. Minus a star because they get overwhelming real quick, and progress slows significantly if you don't use them for boosts. I'm willing to drop a little to avoid having to watch them but still get the boost. Also multiple issues with choosing to watch ads only for the app to not open them, so minus 2 stars for creating a mandatory feature that doesn't work most of the time

Game uses ads for bonuses and let's face it to progress you WILL be watching ads. I can live with ads, what SUCKS is that about 80% of the time ads are not even available! It will take you 5 minutes of refreshing trying to get an ad so you don't lose 8hours of bonus and then you'll give up because it will never work. Have no fear though, that bonus worth 3 minutes of away time will load right away!

The game is okay in terms of story and game play (although I grew up in the 80s, and nobody *I* knew threw buzzwords around quite THAT much). However, upgrading and eventually automating your income at certain stations is dependent upon cards...and getting those cards is randomized unless you spend real money. It's like the controversy over "loot boxes". Not cool.

If you watch an offline ad to double rewards, but the game plans to trigger a cutscene, you end up watching the entire ad and never get credit for it. Has been like this for forever, super annoying. Great game otherwise, aside from lack of content.

It's good for the genre. The animation is good, the gameplay is what you'd expect. The problem is with so many games like this available, it's too easy to find one a little more eye catching. I like it and play it but I have like 5 others that are basically the same thing with flashier skins.

Compared to most games like this, I actually do like this game more. However, it's just a ridiculous thing when it comes to events. PAY-TO-WIN is key for those. You can get top 10-20 if you play regularly and make good upgrading decisions. You'll be lucky if you get 2-10 and don't even think about rank 1 unless your willing to fork out some serious cash.

Update: Disappointed with the balancing. For example, there is very little difference between 2nd and 20th in the events. Original: Overall a pretty good game. It can get to be a bit on the expensive side in both in-game currency and irl currency.

I play games like this as a way to relax and escape for a while. I was enjoying this game until I watched an ad for a bonus and the ad had very bold printed curse words. I don't put up with that from games and I won't put up with it in their ads either so I'm deleting this game. Too bad .. it had potential.

Finally completed the game so ita worth some feedback. Pros - Nice graphics - Nice little story - Mini game inbetween levels us a cool touch - Special events are frequent Cons - No reset for prestige giving the game next to no replayibility - No way to remove ads via in app purchase - Progress on special events is choked massively and is geared towards in app purchases

It's ok. I don't like that there isn't a auto open for the cards. I think its a loss to only have the cereal game to play after the episodes are done! There should be a game from each machine! There needs to be a skip all option for stories

star_4.text: I really enjoy playing this game. Not ad heavy unless you want to watch them, great casual play. Only thing I wish would be improved is the overall information about the cards. Are they given at random or by specific arcade games? How often will each character's cards be available to get? Other than that I love it and the story is fun!

This is an enjoyable and well conceived idle game. I really enjoy the parody nods to iconic 80s mo ies and games. The ads aren't bad at all and completely optional for perks, however there were times when the ads seemed to not work. I found if I clicked the ad to open a browser and backed out that it fixed the problem 8 times out of 10. I have reached level 25 and it seems to have stopped, not story-wise. I reckon the developers are working on tge next levels since the story hasn't concluded.

First impressions..good concept. Progression seems smooth (through first 5 levels at least. Purchases do not seem like good value. Probably will hold off buying until I see if an ad-free purchase option available. Pizza slices need to be more plentiful.

For a clicker game, it's not bad...just like many others on here unfortunately. The graphics and music are awesome - having grown up in the 80s I can definitely appreciate that. However, all of the cheesy, over used, typical 80s movie quotes/references are hugely overdone! I get wanting to sprinkle some 80s pop culture in there, but this is crazy. If you're into the typical clicker game but with an 80s influence, I would recommend this for sure.

This is a good game as far as idle games go. There aren't any ads that pop up out of nowhere, you can choose to watch ads but they aren't that necessary. The events are pretty good and of a nice variety. You can put money in but you don't have to. Just like in real life, you probably won't be 1st on the leader board in the events unless you put up some money, but you can do well enough to get rewarded just playing normally. Not perfect but seems to be actively improving.

Amazing game with a few problems. Out of content for the time. Tons of events but those have upset me now. D and D is big to me and I've spend three days trying to get 5 cards for upgrades I can't even buy. It's disheartening and made me give up.

Love the theme of the game, but an idle game with a similar format. I did run into a bug, which has made my version of the game locked up as of now. But I will continue to play once it's fixed, and I urge anybody who likes idle games to give it a try.
The character list could have a skip to the bottom or five at a time. Otherwise the game is great. There is sound from another game ad on the background today, this needs fixed.

Absolutely love the game the skeeball is indigenous to get extra diamonds. But sometimes you think that some people who are running the top place on contests are running a autotap probably would like to see an anti cheat for this but that's about it overall a fun idle game.
Solid for an idle/afk game. The limited time item bundles are in-your-face annoying, however, and the prices only show right before you purchase. The nostalgia parodies are fun though.
I love the game. One complaint: Make the ad leech a bit clearer in what it does, I got it for an event thinking it would be for the main game as well. 3 bucks is steep for not being permanent.
It is a simple but fun game. Kids love the graphics and 80s type theme. Easy at 1st then slow to grow.
Its not a 5/5 yet, game play is nice so far, and I like the episodes that break up the game so it gets a bit harder as you progress.
Game is like many others but with a great story line. The story will keep you hooked where others fall flat. Lost 1 star for being repetitive.

They finally updated past level 25!!! idk how far this goes but it had been depressing to login and just do side stuff(time sensitive episodes) when all the main stuff was on hold. Episode 26 picks up... forgetting where 25 left off. It makes me think that the creators knew Russia was a sensitive subject again and moved into time travel. My advice for people with a time machine is don't upgrade jack at lvl 25 because you need all of that pizza immediately at 26. Finish the Russia arc please.
Fun I preordered it and it's good but I don't like it when you get a amount of money in a chapter you loose it in the next other than that great game!
great game, no ads, and only one problem, one time I got softlocked and couldn't do anything, but overall, absolutely beautiful game
I played for a few days. The game was fun and I love the 80s nostalgia but it drained my battery. Maybe it's just my phone, I don't know, but that's why I had to delete.
Actually this games not bad. Usual idle game but no forced ads or forced pay to play. After last review I decided to play longer. Went from 1 star to 4.
Nonstop events, Excellent math practice ,and fun characters make this game addictive. Would have given 5 star but only 27 seasons.

star_5.text: Not like an other idle tycoon game!.... Love the story, love the nostalgia and how corny it is 😂. To the developers, to an almost perfect idle game, you're missing just a few things. I would suggest that during events players could go to other players arcades on the leader boards. To see they're setups and compare. Also add a feature that shows total amount the arcade is making. Per sec/min/hr. Those would be huge in determining if a player can catch up to 1st place or not.

I don't normally play mobile games every day, but this one definitely hooked me. As an 80s baby I appreciate all the slang and references, and it definitely gives me that nostalgia of being an arcade rat, hanging out all day and checking out all of the new games (I was definitely Leech!) My only gripe is how difficult it can be to get the cards you need for upgrades, especially in the events. I don't have the money to spend on hundreds of gems and buying card packs, hoping to get what I need.

Absolutely love this game, and I play a ton of idles! Having grown up in the '80s, I really dig the references and the styles in the game. Really loving it. I am wondering if any of your devs worked also on Star Trek: Lower Decks, which only came out late last year... because that game literally copies tons of elements from this game, from layouts and design to upgrades of characters and general gameplay. They just skinned it differently and have different names for items. Curious ;)

DEFINETLY RECCOMEND, for a small game that at the time of typing this has 100k downloads, is a pretty good game and has smooth storyline. The gameplay is amazing, and I'm hooked! I think we have reached a new a Era of "games" hehe. I might even reccomend to a friend. I hope this review helps you as I took my time to type, and you took your time to read.

I didn't know what to expect with this one. I'd played other idle tycoon games and it felt more like ad fests than actual games. This has a story, an amusing sense of humor, plenty of goofy 80s references, mini games, and best of all, minimal (if any!) ads. Definitely give this a go, it's worth it.

Great design, poor performance... I am barely into the second stage and I cannot play anymore... It is asking me to upgrade Jo to level 2, but I don't have the needed items to do so and I can't play the game to get the needed items... Since reboot of the game and recent updates, the game has ran smoothly with no further issues. The devs have been excellent dealing with the issue.

A great little game to kill time that won't take over your life. Main game is fair and not pay-to-win, if you are patient. Some events get difficult, but you can still win gems and prizes without spending a ton. However, I don't mind contributing to such an entertaining game where the devs are clearly having fun building an 80s-centric world full of great characters and cultural references!

I've finished this game a couple months ago. Without a rebirth option, it feels like the coins, cards, etc. are for nothing. The only fun events that haven't felt repetitive are the weekend ones but they're not interesting enough to keep me playing, snd I'm not a big fan of grinding for achievements either so I just stopped playing altogether. Is there actually a way to rebirth? I'd like for the main game to restart but still able to keep my event cards, short of deleting the app to restart.

There are some games that come around, once every several generations, that make you question why you have wasted time playing anything else in life... This is it. This is that game. There is a tactile "feel" to the progression, with future levels' scaling properly as you advance. I especially love the art, music, and sound design! As someone who was born in the 90's, i cannot say that this "brings me back" to the 80's, but it makes me feel like I'm right there! 11/10, would recommend!!!!!!!!

This game is tops 😻 It plays like an interactive graphic novel with great storylines👍 & if you're of a certain age and love this 80s/90s resurgence, it's got what you need! That's why it's the only idle game I haven't gotten bored with & uninstalled. Thanks to the whole development team 🙏 Now this is where you, the reader, emphatically say "Thanks, now we know!👍", to which I'll cleverly reply "And knowing is half the battle!"😉

very nice & very casual game! a lot like the setup from a few other games available but this is a much more "generation specific" to my age group (42 yrs of age) which is the deciding factor for this game being tops of the lists! Great work, Devs! lookin' forward to more updates as soon as possible!

Love the game but noticed the insane amount of bots that invade the game and take the top spot prize is out of control. I have decided to see who my competitors are. I have held the top spot before and noticed out of nowhere someone who wasn't even on the board the night before all of a sudden not only has the top spot but also is ridiculously in front of everyone else. Unless your spending $50+ its impossible to catch up.

If you don't like idle games, this is probably not going to change your mind. If you do, this game has more personality than any other I have tried. Do the cut scenes interfere with absolute maximizing, probably, but they're so dumb in such an adorable way. It's not going to be everyone's vibe, but it's mine. There is an actual story and characters, it's an idle with a world. A goofy, dumb, meta, 80's to the point I dont see how anyone under 40 could really get, IP infringement avoiding, world.

Excellent progressive clicker, I love the caracters, the story is humorous and original, and the app doesn't beat you over the head with ad spam, so it's more relaxing than other similar games, best new idle game I've played in a hot minute!

You don't need to explain how to play the events so thoroughly every single event. I think after playing a hundred of the we get it. It's clunky and slow to have to tap each pop up to close them. Otherwise I truly enjoy your game thanks.

Cute graphics and storyline. The writer's attempts at using 80's & 90's slang made me laugh a couple of times. Wait times like most Idle games with currency build are quick-paced in the beginning however with difficulty improving over each new mission fair warning they increase depending on the severity of the mission.

This game is very fun, and even though it is new, you can tell that the devs put a lot of work into this. The storyline is fun, the characters are well made with great personalites, and the game makes you feel as though you are running your own arcade!

I love this game. I participate in every event but my only issue is once you hit level 25, there isn't anything left to do in the main story. I would love more story levels to make the event rewards be more useful.

This game is an absolute GEM💎 this is unlike any isle game that I have played it has a really nice storyline that I love. The games him or is on another level I just love this game. there are little to no ads and the ads are not intrusive.I hope this game grows even more. A big thank you to the devs, thank you for producing such a epic game 😁😁

The game is a fun way to past the time. I'm currently trying this on the new google play beta client for PC and haven't run into any issue and the game doesn't have a lot of ads. Overall, a pretty awesome game.
